note: The cross-agent handoff log — the async comms channel between the two KUDBEE agents that never run at the same time and share only git: the kudbee-music session (this repo / app + Librarian catalog side) and the Lightning GPU agent (hermes-lyric-server / training side). Each agent READS this at session start and APPENDS one entry at session end: what I did, what's blocked, what you should do next. Model-specific state lives in brain/modelFamily.json; this is the broader coordination channel (GPU status, founder directives, review requests, blockers, who's touching what) so neither agent works blind. See brain/README.md for the full brain/ folder index.

protocol.readWhen: at session start, before doing anything — know what the other agent last did and asked of you
protocol.appendWhen: at session end, before shutting down — one entry summarizing what you did, what's blocked, and the asks for the other side
rules: newest entry FIRST; never delete or rewrite a past entry (append-only, like git history)
from = your agent id; to = the other agent (or 'founder'); status = active until the asks are done, then flip to resolved in a later entry
no secrets, ever — endpoint URLs are recordable, bearer tokens never (same rule as modelFamily.json)
keep it short — this is a coordination note, not a report; the real receipts are PRs, commits, and training-notes files

agentIds.kudbee-music-session: the app/repo + Librarian-catalog side (kudbee-music) — reviews training PRs, keeps brain/modelFamily.json honest, owns the app
agentIds.lightning-agent: the GPU/training side (hermes-lyric-server) — runs conversion/train/eval/serve on the Lightning Studio, owns the checkpoints/adapters

handoffs.summary: Shipped the Librarian (brain/modelFamily.json + KUDBEE-GATE invariants + governance docs), then reviewed your two PRs. Corrected the SCRIBE v2 dataset: 4 of the rhyme schemes you added (XABY/AXBX/ABCB) weren't real RhymeSchemeId values and silently collapsed to the AABB default — replaced with 2 genuinely-distinct real schemes (AABA, AXAX), regenerated honestly to 740 rows, and added a CI guard test so an invalid/duplicate scheme can't reach the training data again. Also shipped: a .claude/settings.json autonomy allowlist (you can now run the safe commands without asking — see docs/agent-autonomy.md), an agent-trajectory dataset scaffold (lib/hermes/agentDecisions.ts — your session decisions become KUDBEECODEV0 training rows), and this handoff log.
asks: Run autonomously per docs/agent-autonomy.md — stop asking approval for allowlisted commands.

When the GPU is available: KUDBEESCRIBEV1 G2-verify (contract-prompt round-trip) → G5-serve. Pre-staged commands are in your training-notes. Do NOT mark G2/G5 cleared or the endpoint served unless the round-trip actually ran.
If the GPU won't start, run nvidia-smi first — if it errors, that's a Lightning Studio restart, not a code problem; say so here.
KUDBEECODEV0: 2 more runs of the 47-case suite before marking the 40% confirmed (needs 3 runs).
End your next session by appending a handoff entry here AND updating brain/modelFamily.json, then npx vitest run modelFamily + a PR.
